---
name: "Doxygen Documentation"
description: "Guidelines for adding Doxygen documentation to C++ headers in the nanoipc repository."
version: "1.0"
---

# Skill: Doxygen Documentation

Guidelines for adding Doxygen documentation to C++ headers in this repository.

## Comment style

Use **triple-slash** (`///`) line comments for all Doxygen annotations.  
Do **not** use `/** ... */` block comments.

```cpp
/// @brief Short one-line description.
```

## Classes and structs

- Add `@brief` with a short description on the line immediately before the class/struct definition.
- Add `@tparam` for every template parameter, directly after `@brief`.

```cpp
/// @brief Reads COBS-framed byte vectors from a Linux UART device.
/// @tparam N Capacity of the internal ring buffer in bytes.
template <std::size_t N>
class LinuxUartCobsFrameReader : public Reader<std::vector<std::uint8_t>> {
```

## Methods

Document **only public** methods. Use `@brief` for a short description.  
Add `@param` for each parameter and `@return` when the return type is non-void.

```cpp
/// @brief Reads one complete COBS frame, if available.
/// @return The decoded frame payload, or std::nullopt if no complete frame is ready yet.
std::optional<std::vector<std::uint8_t>> read() override;

/// @brief Constructs a reader bound to the given UART device.
/// @param uart Pointer to an open serialib instance. Must not be null.
explicit LinuxUartCobsFrameReader(serialib *uart);
```

## What NOT to document

- **Default constructors / destructors** generated by the compiler.
- **Deleted copy/move constructors and assignment operators.**
- **Private members and methods.**
- Implementation `.cpp` files — document the header declaration only.
